title: Preparation of chemically competent E. coli cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"943qu","text":"Standard protocol to prepare cells for highly efficient DNA uptake using CaCl2  (Sambrook and Russell, Molecular Cloning: A Laboratory Manual, 2001). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":77,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Carefully suspend each pellet in 20 ml of icecold 100 m CaCl2 and incubate on ice for 1 h. 01:00:00
Inoculate a 5 ml overnight-culture in LB-medium without antibiotics.
Inoculate 100 ml LB-medium with 1/100 volume of the overnight culture and incubate to an OD 600 = 0.5 - 0.6 at 37 °C and 230 rpm.
Transfer cells to two 50 ml sterile falcon tubes and incubate on ice for 20 min. From this step on, keep cells always cold. 00:20:00
Centrifuge for 5 min at 4500 rpm and 4 °C (pre-cool centrifuge) and discard the supernatant. 00:05:00
Carefully suspend each pellet in 2 ml icecold 85 mM CaCl2 containing 15% Glycerol.
Centrifuge for 5 min at 4500 rpm and 4 °C (pre-cool centrifuge) and discard the supernatant. 00:05:00

Pipette 100 to 200 µl aliquots in 1.5 ml tubes (work on ice) and store at -80 °C. (you can shock-freeze the cells in liquid nitrogen or in dry ice prior to storage at -80°C, but it is not required) For DNA transformation, thaw 100 µl of the competent cells on ice.
creator: Alice Pawlowski
